title: Create eval scenario writing best practices doc
description: Key decisions from batch 2-5 session need to be documented as scenario writing guidelines. Include: failing-test requirement for seed workspaces, prompt design principle (prompt must make it nearly impossible to complete without triggering lesson), Form A/B both need control arms, SKIP pattern diagnosis (P1: completed seed, P2: invisible tool calls, P3: trigger not reached), CONTROL_CORRECT pressure testing workflow. Create docs/eval-scenario-writing.md and link from docs/eval-usage.md.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add eval env vars to CLAUDE.md (meridian proxy required)
description: Every eval run must be prefixed with ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=meridian ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:3456 or the judge and agent calls will fail silently. This is not documented anywhere in CLAUDE.md or the eval README. Add a Running Evals section to CLAUDE.md before the Running Tests section.
close_reason: Closed

title: Eval Framework Phase 1: pnpm workspace skeleton
description: Implement Phase 1 of PRD 004 eval framework. Set up evals/ as pnpm workspace. Deliver: pnpm-workspace.yaml at root, evals/package.json (Promptfoo + helpers), evals/promptfooconfig.yaml, providers/claude-agent.mjs + codex-agent.mjs stubs, scripts/materialize-workspace.mjs, scripts/render-report.mjs, results/cache and results/reports dirs, root package.json eval scripts. End state: npm run eval:smoke errors with 'no scenarios found'. Decisions: no Docker (temp dirs), single-prompt dialog injection, pnpm workspaces, content-hash arm caching, shared intervention type field.
close_reason: Phase 1 skeleton complete and pushed. pnpm workspace, Promptfoo provider, materialize-workspace, collect-artifacts, render-report, justfile, CI updated. eval:smoke exits 0 with 0 tests as expected.

title: Purge 406 heuristic garbage candidates from DB
description:
lessons review is unusable: 415 candidates total, 406 are Tier 2 heuristic garbage (confidence 0.55, raw terminal output). Only 8 are real (confidence >= 0.8).

Options:
1. Bulk archive via 'lessons promote --ids ... --action archive' for all below threshold
2. Add 'lessons purge --below-conf 0.6' subcommand for future use

Goal: make lessons review usable again by clearing the garbage tier.
close_reason: Added 'lessons purge --below-conf' command; archived 406 garbage candidates (conf < 0.6); 9 real candidates remain

title: Rearchitect semantic scanner: move extraction to lessons review
description:
The background scanner currently calls Anthropic API (extractWithClaude) to extract lessons from flagged windows. This is wrong architecture — no API key should be required in a background process.

What needs to change:
1. Remove extractWithClaude from scanner entirely
2. Store flagged windows (dist < 0.72) as pending_semantic status in DB with raw window text
3. Surface pending_semantic candidates in 'lessons review' for Claude to extract inline
4. No API key needed in background scanner

This unblocks the scanner from requiring an API key and makes extraction interactive (Claude does it during review).
close_reason: Removed extractWithClaude from semantic scanner. Added pending_semantic_windows table and insertPendingWindow/getPendingWindows/archivePendingWindows DB functions. Added 'lessons windows' subcommand. Pending windows surface in 'lessons review'. No API key needed in background scanner.

title: Add tests + banner + /lessons:handoff command for anti-compact feature
description: Tests for precompact-handoff.mjs (unit + integration), context-full banner on disabled path, LESSONS_HANDOFF_ONLY mode for on-demand handoffs, /lessons:handoff command with auto/on/off subcommands.
close_reason: Extracted pure functions to hooks/lib/precompact.mjs, added LESSONS_HANDOFF_ONLY mode, context-full banner on disabled path, 24 unit tests + 13 integration tests, /lessons:handoff command

title: Pre-compact hook: emit token count + fenced handoff prompt before /compact fires
description:
Wire a PreCompact hook (investigate if Claude Code supports this event — check hooks.json event types and Claude Code docs) that intercepts /compact before it runs. Hook emits: current token count + a fenced markdown copy-paste handoff prompt summarizing session state (active issues, recent changes, next steps) so the user can paste into a new session and continue without context loss. All emitted prompts must be fenced markdown blocks. Trial in this project first; extract to standalone plugin if successful.

FALLBACK CONDITION: If PreCompact fires too late (context already severely degraded by the time the hook runs), fall back to earlier-trigger research. Options: (1) systemd/launchd service monitoring session JSONL for token growth and alerting before degradation; (2) in-process token counting heuristic in the hook layer with a configurable threshold; (3) scheduled compact trigger. Only pursue fallback path if pre-compact hook timing proves insufficient.
close_reason: Created hooks/precompact-handoff.mjs + added PreCompact entry to hooks.json; blocks compaction with quality handoff via claude -p + fallback

title: Fix claude-mem MCP: memory not being captured or injected
description: claude-mem (thedotmack plugin v10.5.2) shows prompts in dashboard but creates no memories. Root causes identified: (1) mcp-search MCP server defined in plugin .mcp.json but NOT registered in ~/.claude/settings.json mcpServers — Claude Code tools unavailable; (2) Chroma vector DB at ~/Library/Application Support/mcp-memory/ last modified Aug 2025, suggesting PostToolUse observation hook is silently failing; (3) no error logging on hook failures. Fix: register MCP server explicitly in settings.json, add hook error logging, verify DB write access.
close_reason: Registered mcp-search MCP server in settings.json and merged all claude-mem hooks (SessionStart, UserPromptSubmit, PostToolUse, Stop) with absolute fallback paths

title: Discover PXE server layout and fill in pi-* script placeholders
description: SSH into pxe server, capture df/ls/exports/services output. Fill in NFS_ROOT, TFTP_ROOT, PXE_SERVER in ~/bin/pi-bind-mounts; NODES in ~/bin/pi-status; NODE_IP/NODE_ROLE in ~/bin/pi-cloud-init. Update pi-cluster.md Cluster State table with real paths.
close_reason: PXE layout fully discovered: TFTP=/tftpboot, NFS=/tftpboot/images, pi5-1/pi5-2 confirmed PXE booting, all scripts filled in

title: Preserve EEPROM recovery image: copy from /tmp to /var/lib/pxe/images + local Mac
description: The recovery image at /tmp/eeprom-recovery-sd/ on the PXE server is in a 449MB tmpfs and will be lost on reboot. Copy to /var/lib/pxe/images (persistent) and scp to ~/Downloads/pi-eeprom/ locally for flashing.
notes: Recovery image was lost from /tmp before session. pi5-1 and pi5-2 already have correct EEPROM (0xf21). Image only needed if pi5-3 requires EEPROM recovery. Use pi-eeprom-recovery to re-download if needed.
close_reason: Not recoverable (tmpfs cleared), but pi5-1/pi5-2 EEPROM already correct. pi5-3 status TBD.

title: Fix TC-H51: suppressOutput:true FAIL — lesson solution not prescriptive enough
description:
TC-H51 (suppressOutput-true-in-hook-js) returned FAIL in batch6. Judge finding: treatment agent did not apply the lesson solution — instead of removing suppressOutput:true from the hook output, it incorrectly claimed the task was already complete based on a misread. Judge recommendation: make the lesson solution more prescriptive.

Root cause to investigate: is the prompt too ambiguous? Does the lesson solution explicitly say to remove the field or just describe the problem? Check lesson body and PROMPT.md to determine if the fix is in the scenario prompt or the lesson text itself.

Fix: edit lesson solution to be more prescriptive (explicit 'remove suppressOutput:true from the JSON output object' instruction), or tighten the PROMPT.md to force the agent to actually write/modify the hook file.

title: Add CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR isolation to eval agent runs
description: Eval agents currently inherit the user's global Claude Code settings, which means lessons from the user's own store can leak into control arms. Fix: set CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR to a temp dir in claude-agent.mjs so each eval agent gets a clean global config. Caveat: workspace-local .claude/ in the seed workspace is still read — manage that separately per scenario if project-level hooks are needed.
close_reason: Set CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR=$HOME/.claude (in fake eval home) alongside existing HOME override in buildEnv(). Both now point at the same empty temp dir. Committed in feat(evals) 4c59b1a.

title: Manual transcript review for TC-H38 regression (SKIP, delta=-3.6)
description: Large treatment regression (-3.6) even though judge couldn't call FAIL. Treatment agent wrote incorrect bash (2>&1 >&1 2>/dev/tty) before recovering — the lesson about DeprecationWarnings confused it when the task was actually about stdout/stderr redirection. The scenario redesign issue (tracked separately) should fix the root cause. After redesign, re-run and verify delta improves.
close_reason: TC-H38 re-run with redesigned scenario: CONTROL_CORRECT (delta=+0.4) — agent suppresses DeprecationWarnings without the lesson. Scenario design is now correct; CONTROL_CORRECT is a valid signal (lesson not needed by a capable agent).

title: Investigate TC-H41 lesson: patch or retire (FAIL, delta=-2.6)
description: Treatment arm scored worse than control. Lesson text may be confusing or counterproductive. Full analysis done: the scenario is broken (no seed file, Serena not available, prompt contradicts lesson) which is tracked separately. This issue is specifically about whether the lesson text itself is clear and actionable once the scenario is fixed. After TC-H41 scenario fix is done, re-run and evaluate whether the lesson delta improves.
close_reason: Implemented in fix(evals) commit a6333fc: mock-serena.mjs created, lesson toolNames fixed, TC-H38 redesigned with cli.js + new prompt + new verify, match.mjs patched for tool-name-only matching

title: Fix TC-D9: create seed workspace with committed git history
description: TC-D9 (asking-should-i-proceed-on-routine-reversible) has an empty seed workspace — agent correctly identified a blocker but couldn't demonstrate lesson behavior because there was nothing to act on. Fix: create seed workspace with actual source files committed in git history. Task should require a straightforward reversible change (e.g., rename snake_case functions to camelCase in src/utils.js). Agent needs to see committed history to know the action is reversible.

title: Fix TC-D8: replace claude --print with Agent SDK + mock canUseTool
description: AskUserQuestion is available in Claude Code but requires a canUseTool callback that claude --print does not provide, so the agent correctly falls back to prose. The only way to test actual AskUserQuestion usage is via the Agent SDK with a mock canUseTool that returns the first/recommended option automatically. Replace the claude --print provider for TC-D8 with an Agent SDK call that: (1) mocks canUseTool returning true for AskUserQuestion, (2) auto-selects the first option when the tool is invoked, (3) captures whether AskUserQuestion was actually called. This is the correct test for whether the agent uses the tool at all.

title: Fix TC-D6: use bleeding-edge API scenario (training data would produce wrong code)
description: TC-D6 (planning-from-training-data-knowledge) is hard to verify if task uses a stable API the agent knows well. Fix: replace prompt with a task requiring a recently-added API where training data would produce wrong or outdated code. Good candidates: next/form (Next.js 15), use cache directive (Next.js 15 experimental), useOptimistic changed signature, or a lessons-learned/beads-specific API that is completely post-training. Prompt pattern: 'add X feature to this project' where X requires a specific recent API.

title: Fix TC-D4: patch lesson timing + rewrite scenario to planning stage
description: Lesson says 'search for known pitfalls before committing to a plan' but the correct timing (confirmed by user) is AFTER forming a plan, BEFORE presenting it. Two fixes: (1) patch lesson solution to specify timing: 'after you have a plan, before you present it, search for known issues with your approach'. (2) Rewrite scenario PROMPT.md to put agent at planning stage — they already have a rough approach and are about to present it, not still gathering requirements.

title: Fix Pattern 3 scenarios: prompt doesn't reach trigger point (H5, H18, H23)
description: Three scenarios fail because the prompt never puts the agent in a position to trigger the lesson. TC-H5 (VSCode signing): change prompt from describing GPG symptoms to 'configure VSCode git.enableCommitSigning setting'. TC-H18 (batch rollback): rework prompt to start mid-execution ('here is the approved plan, execute it') so rollback lesson triggers naturally rather than agent stopping at clarification questions. TC-H23 (decorator registry): create seed workspace with an actual registry module that has the missing import the lesson addresses.

title: Add PostToolUse hook for tool-call observability in eval scenarios (Pattern 2)
description: Some scenarios SKIP because the agent's actual tool calls aren't visible in the transcript — can't confirm pytest flags used, can't see which tools a subagent picked. Fix: add a PostToolUse hook to each scenario seed workspace .claude/settings.json that appends every tool call + input to .eval/tool-calls.jsonl. Update judge.mjs and verify.mjs to read this log for trajectory checks instead of scraping transcript text. Hook config: { hooks: { PostToolUse: [{ matcher: '*', hooks: [{ type: 'command', command: "jq -c '{tool: .tool_name, input: .tool_input}' >> .eval/tool-calls.jsonl" }] }] } }

title: Fix Pattern 1 SKIP scenarios: strip seed workspaces + add failing tests (D7, H6, H9-H12, H16)
description: 7 scenarios SKIP because seed workspace already contains completed code — agent summarizes prior work instead of solving anything. Fix for each: (1) strip seed workspace back to broken/incomplete starting state, (2) add a test that fails in the starting state and passes when the lesson's fix is applied, (3) verify the test fails locally before committing. Scenarios: TC-D7, TC-H6, TC-H9, TC-H10, TC-H11, TC-H12, TC-H16.

title: Add control arms to all Form B scenarios (D1, D3, D5, H2, P2-P6)
description: Form B scenarios (protocol/directive lessons) currently only run the treatment arm. This is the TDD red-test problem — without a control arm, a PASS doesn't tell you whether the agent would have demonstrated the behavior anyway. Add control arm entries to promptfooconfig.yaml for: TC-D1, TC-D3, TC-D5, TC-H2, TC-P2, TC-P3, TC-P4, TC-P5, TC-P6. Current Form B PASSes that may be vacuous: D1, D3, D5, H2, P2, P3, P4, P5, P6.

title: TC-H38: Redesign scenario to actually trigger DeprecationWarnings
description: TC-H38 scenario prompt (write a bash script for stdout/stderr redirection) has nothing to do with Node.js DeprecationWarnings. The lesson fires because node appears in the command but the task never involves warnings. Treatment agent got confused and wrote incorrect bash (2>&1 >&1 2>/dev/tty). Fix: create seed workspace with a Node.js script that uses deprecated APIs (e.g. require('punycode') or new Buffer()) that reliably emits DeprecationWarning to stderr. New prompt: run this script and capture clean output to output.txt without the DeprecationWarning noise. Verify: check that the agent's command includes NODE_NO_WARNINGS=1 or --no-deprecation.
close_reason: Implemented in fix(evals) commit a6333fc: mock-serena.mjs created, lesson toolNames fixed, TC-H38 redesigned with cli.js + new prompt + new verify, match.mjs patched for tool-name-only matching

title: TC-H41: Create mock Serena MCP + fix lesson toolNames trigger
description: TC-H41 is untestable without Serena in the eval environment. Two fixes needed: (1) Create evals/tools/mock-serena.mjs — a lightweight MCP server that implements replace_symbol_body with the intentional const double-declaration bug so the lesson has a real mistake to prevent. Configure it in the scenario seed workspace .claude/settings.json. (2) Fix the lesson trigger: toolNames is currently [Bash] with a commandPattern matching the MCP tool name as a Bash command — this can never fire. Change toolNames to [mcp__plugin_serena_serena__replace_symbol_body]. Also add src/config/constants.js with MAX_RETRY_COUNT=3 to the seed workspace, and remove 'Use Serena for the edit' from the prompt (which directly contradicts the lesson).
close_reason: Implemented in fix(evals) commit a6333fc: mock-serena.mjs created, lesson toolNames fixed, TC-H38 redesigned with cli.js + new prompt + new verify, match.mjs patched for tool-name-only matching

title: Add lessons from Vercel/GitHub App PKCS#1 OpenSSL 3 post-mortem
description: Two production gotchas from the claude-review-bot post-mortem need lessons: (1) GitHub App PKCS#1 keys silently fail on Node 20+/OpenSSL 3 (Vercel) because createPrivateKey() throws ERR_OSSL_UNSUPPORTED after the 202 is sent — convert to PKCS#8 before storing. (2) vercel logs silently filters by branch; --no-branch is required to see production logs.
close_reason: Closed

title: Design and implement 'duplicated_by' field: suppress lessons when superseded
description: New lesson field `duplicatedBy` with two sub-use-cases: (1) Suppression by installed artifact — if a named skill/plugin/MCP server is present, exclude the lesson from the manifest (e.g., don't inject brainstorming protocol if superpowers plugin is installed). If the artifact is later removed, reactivate the lesson. (2) Upstream issue shim — if a lesson is a temporary fix for a known upstream bug, record the GitHub issue URL. Doctor checks issue status; when closed + fix version confirmed deployed, archives the lesson permanently. Doctor check covers both cases. Requires defining what 'installed' means for each artifact type (skill: file in ~/.claude/skills/, plugin: ~/.claude/plugins/, MCP: ~/.claude/settings.json mcpServers key).
close_reason: Closed

title: Refine two lessons: add question-vs-task distinction
description: bias-toward-action and asking-should-i-proceed correctly target task assignment but misfire when the user is asking a question. Both lessons need problem/solution updates to make the authorization boundary explicit.
close_reason: Closed

title: Implement Promptfoo-native Tier 2+3 eval framework
description:
Implement the full Promptfoo-native eval framework designed in session 2026-05-10. Plan: ~/.claude/plans/lessons-learned-session-giggly-beacon.md

Design decisions locked in:
1. Promptfoo is the eval system — no leval CLI, no eval.db, no lesson-test-scenarios.json
2. Tier 3 judge runs inside claude-agent.mjs provider for treatment arms
3. Two cache keys: control_hash (triggerPrompt+model) and arm_cache_key (triggerPrompt+model+lessonProblem+lessonSolution)
4. Form A (hint/guard): judge sees control + treatment, computes delta; Form B (protocol/directive): judge sees treatment only
5. CONTROL_CORRECT: always prompt-quality-first, archival second
6. before-each.mjs injects lessonSnapshot into test vars; after-all.mjs outputs failure guidance
7. maxConcurrency: 1 + all controls before treatments to guarantee control cache
8. npm run eval:gen scaffolds TC-* folders, updates promptfooconfig.yaml
9. npx promptfoo mcp registered as stdio MCP server in .mcp.json
10. /eval slash command in commands/eval.md
11. interventionType field for extensibility (skills, CLAUDE.md, prompts)

Files to create:
- evals/scripts/judge.mjs
- evals/scripts/assert-judge.mjs
- evals/scripts/hooks/before-each.mjs
- evals/scripts/hooks/after-all.mjs
- evals/scripts/generate-scenarios.mjs
- commands/eval.md
- evals/README.md

Files to modify:
- evals/providers/claude-agent.mjs — control cache + judge call
- evals/promptfooconfig.yaml — extensions, maxConcurrency, reorder tests, assert-judge
- evals/scenarios/TC-*/scenario.json — add lessonId + interventionType fields
- evals/scripts/render-report.mjs — dimension scores + CONTROL_CORRECT sections
- evals/package.json — eval:gen, eval:runs scripts, @anthropic-ai/sdk dep
- .mcp.json — register promptfoo mcp
close_reason: Closed

title: Add Tier 2 + Tier 3 grading to Promptfoo eval framework
description:
The existing Promptfoo eval framework (Phase 4 complete, 18/18 arms passing) uses only Tier 1 (deterministic verify.mjs checks). Add Tier 2 trajectory assertions only — the full Tier 3 LLM judge is designed and will be implemented as part of the new Promptfoo-native eval framework issue (see implementation issue blocked by this one).

Tier 2 — Trajectory grading:
- Parse hook events from .eval/hook-events.ndjson
- Assert that the correct sequence of tool calls occurred
- e.g. for guard lessons: blocked command → correct alternative used
- Artifacts already collected (collect-artifacts.mjs exists), just not used for grading

Files to touch:
- evals/scripts/assert-trajectory.mjs (new file, trajectory assertion logic)
- evals/promptfooconfig.yaml (wire trajectory assertions into treatment arms)
- evals/scripts/render-report.mjs (include trajectory failures in failure blocks)

Note: Tier 3 (LLM judge) is NOT in scope here — it is fully designed in the Promptfoo-native eval framework plan and will be implemented separately.
close_reason: assert-trajectory.mjs implemented with required/forbidden rule engine; 5 scenarios wired with meaningful trajectory assertions; all 9 treatment arms in promptfooconfig.yaml updated; render-report.mjs surfaces trajectory failures in failure blocks

title: feat(evals): Phase 2 — TC-H3 and TC-G1 first real scenarios
description: Phase 2 of PRD 004 eval framework. Implement the first two real eval scenarios (TC-H3 hardcoded-secrets hint, TC-G1 pytest-hang guard), fix manifest object/array bug in materialize-workspace, add agent-output logging and hidden-check execution to provider, and implement sha256 arm result caching.
close_reason: Completed and pushed as commit 8f98e0b. All Phase 2 deliverables shipped.

title: Group lesson candidates by tag in review/approval output
description: When lessons review surfaces candidates, group them by shared tags instead of DB insertion order. Reviewers working thematically can spot overlapping or redundant lessons more easily, deprioritize or skip duplicates, and move faster through related items. Tag grouping mirrors the planned injection grouping (tag-cluster injection issue) for consistency.
close_reason: cmdReview now groups candidates by primary tag; tagged groups sorted alphabetically, untagged last; all 236 tests pass

title: Add authoritative preamble before injected lesson list at session start
description: Prepend a short framing paragraph before the lesson list in session-start injection that establishes them as high-priority, hard-won rules — similar to how superpowers skills use authoritative, hard-gate framing. Goal: the model treats them as non-negotiable without every individual lesson needing to repeat that framing. Reference the superpowers:using-superpowers skill for framing examples.
close_reason: Split directive vs protocol preambles in session-start-lesson-protocol.mjs — directives get IMPORTANT wrapper, protocols get coordination framing

title: Group session-start lessons by tag clusters in injection output
description: Instead of emitting lessons in DB order at session start, cluster them by shared tags (tool:X, severity:Y, topic:Z) so the model receives thematically coherent blocks. Explore multi-tag overlap handling (e.g., a lesson matching two clusters — assign to primary tag or emit in both).
close_reason: groupByTag helper added; directives and protocols now cluster by primary tag with ### sub-headers when >1 group; single-group sections unchanged; 236 tests pass

title: Create lesson nursery: holding area for lessons with no viable injection path yet
description:
Some lessons encode valid knowledge but can't be injected yet because the current matching system doesn't support their trigger pattern (no content-based matching, no project-type scoping). Rather than leaving them as noisy global protocols or deleting them, create a designated place for collecting these cases.

What the nursery is:
- A status value or tag that marks a lesson as excluded from injection
- Lessons are preserved for pattern discovery and future use
- Accumulating examples will surface patterns for new injection mechanisms

Current nursery candidates:
- process-management (ex: child.unref): needs content-aware matching (detect spawn/Popen patterns in source being edited)
- local-plugin: needs project-type scoping (detect: is this repo a Claude Code plugin?)

Open questions:
- Status value ('nursery') vs tag ('in:nursery') vs separate table?
- How does bd list / bd doctor report nursery contents?
- Should nursery lessons still appear in bd list or be hidden?
- What's the graduation criteria to move out of nursery?

Related: lessons-learned-5ny (injection archetypes investigation)
close_reason: GitHub issue opened at https://github.com/joeblackwaslike/lessons-learned/issues/1; two lessons archived to nursery with in:nursery tag

title: Lesson citation format: include summary+problem+solution when referencing multiple lessons
description: When Claude references multiple lessons by ID in a single response, the output often just lists IDs (e.g. 'see lesson #42, #43') with no inline content. Readers can't act on bare IDs. Citations should inline the summary+problem+solution so the context is visible without a separate lookup.
close_reason: problem+solution now flow through manifest→Match→select fallback citation; budget-exceeded lessons show summary+problem+solution instead of summary only; makeMatch fixture updated; 236 tests pass

title: Add serial console guidance to pi-cluster.md skill
description: pi-cluster.md has no mention of serial console. Serial is the fastest debug tool — shows BOOT_ORDER and exact failure point immediately, while SSH/ping/dnsmasq checks give no signal when a node fails to boot. Document: device /dev/cu.usbmodem112402, baud 115200, non-interactive read pattern, and rule: always check serial first when a node fails to boot or reach the network.
close_reason: Added serial console section to /Users/joe/.agents/skills/pi-cluster.md: device path, baud 115200, screen/cat commands, hardware serial codes table, and the 'check serial first' rule.

title: lessons-learned plugin features: /help command, /onboard command, doctor automation
description: Session 41624768 was actively building these features but ended with 'continue' prompts without confirmed completion. Features: (1) /help slash command for config/command discovery; (2) /onboard command to load initial lessons from JSON with per-lesson approval workflow; (3) doctor command automated scheduling (run weekly, auto-trigger after /lessons:review). Verify which are implemented vs only designed before starting.
close_reason: Implemented: /help already existed. /onboard: new CLI subcommand + command file. Doctor auto-trigger already in review.md Phase 8. Weekly scheduling filed as lessons-learned-y0d.

title: Tag/schema taxonomy audit: rename types, fix 13 hints→directives
description: Session 27f83720 did a comprehensive audit of lesson types and schema consistency but was interrupted. Key findings: 13 'hint' lessons should be reclassified as 'directive' type; field renames under discussion (mistake→problem, remediation→solution). The audit examined lessons.schema.json vs manifest.schema.json alignment. Work was paused mid-discussion of why the 13 hints-to-directives reclassification matters.
close_reason: Reclassified 8 behavioral hints as directives. Field renames were already done. 11 directives total, 47 hints, 193 tests passing.

title: Set up PXE infrastructure: NFS root export + TFTP boot files per node
description: Configure NFS root filesystem per node under NFS_ROOT/<node>/. Add to /etc/exports. Populate TFTP dirs by Pi serial (short serial = last 8 hex chars). Verify bind mounts with pi-bind-mounts.
close_reason: duplicate of lessons-learned-283

title: Set up PXE infrastructure: NFS root export + TFTP boot files per node
description: Configure NFS root filesystem per node under NFS_ROOT/<node>/. Add to /etc/exports. Populate TFTP dirs by Pi serial (short serial = last 8 hex chars). Verify bind mounts with pi-bind-mounts.
close_reason: All three complete: EEPROM 0xf21 confirmed on pi5-3, PXE booting as pi-3, cloud-init status done

title: Flash EEPROM recovery image on pi1, pi2, pi3
description: Two-flash procedure: flash 2712-sd.img to SD card, boot each Pi (green LED pattern then auto-off), remove SD. Verify EEPROM bootloader_config shows network in BOOT_ORDER. Requires physical access to each node.
notes: Session 5eb75583 was interrupted on Apr 17 02:43 UTC. pi5-1 and pi5-2 are DONE (BOOT_ORDER=0xf21, online). pi5-3 (serial: 6b91f29e) is the only remaining node. Recovery SD card may still be inserted — physical state unknown. Recovery image: rpi-boot-eeprom-recovery-*-2712-sd.img (NOT 2711). Target: BOOT_ORDER=0xf21. Verify with: vcgencmd bootloader_config | grep BOOT_ORDER via Pi Debug Probe serial console.
close_reason: All three complete: EEPROM 0xf21 confirmed on pi5-3, PXE booting as pi-3, cloud-init status done

title: Custom eval viewer: full-width transcripts, pretty-printed lesson YAML, judge reasoning
description: Built-in promptfoo viewer is not extensible and has a tiny detail pane. A custom viewer reading from evals/results/cache/ would provide: full-width detail view per scenario, rendered Markdown transcript, lesson snapshot as pretty-printed YAML (currently minified JSON in metadata), judge reasoning formatted, links to validate trajectory, GitHub-style artifact viewer for files written during eval. ROI is high given time spent on manual analysis.

title: Pressure test CONTROL_CORRECT candidates (G2, G3, G4, H3, H4, H8, H13, H20, H22, H28, H33, H34, H39)
description:
13 scenarios returned CONTROL_CORRECT and 1 FAIL where control already avoided the mistake — the control arm avoids the mistake without the lesson. These may indicate the lesson is no longer needed for current model versions. Process: run each scenario 5 times without the lesson injection. If all 5 pass CONTROL_CORRECT, archive the lesson with reason: 'model no longer makes this mistake as of claude-sonnet-4-6, confirmed via 5x pressure test'. Keep archived lessons for regression testing on future models.

Candidates from batch 2-5 (CONTROL_CORRECT): G2, G3, G4, H3, H4, H8, H13, H20, H22, H28, H33, H34, H39
Candidates from batch 6 (FAIL, control already avoided): TC-H42 xargs-word-splits (slug: xargs-word-splits-filenames)

title: Add meta-check to doctor: scan for missing or stale checks given current lessons
description: Doctor currently runs a fixed set of static checks. Add a meta-check that reviews the current lesson corpus and surfaces additional checks that would be relevant but are not currently implemented. This could be heuristic (e.g., detect lesson patterns that imply a check exists but isn't wired) or LLM-assisted (pass lesson list to Claude, ask what doctor checks are missing). Goal: doctor stays current as the lesson library grows.
close_reason: 4 heuristic store-level meta-checks added to auditStore: uncovered-tools, tool-concentration, blanket-bash, untagged-majority. Calibrated against real 99-lesson corpus. 6 integration tests added. Commit efc209c.

title: Implement 'requires' field: lesson only injects when named skill/plugin/tool is installed
description: Opposite of duplicated_by. A new `requires` field on lessons causes the lesson to be excluded from the manifest unless a named artifact (skill, plugin, MCP server) is detected as installed. Use case: a lesson that teaches how to use a specific skill (e.g., superpowers:brainstorming) only makes sense if that skill is present. Artifact detection logic can reuse whatever is built for duplicated_by.
close_reason: Closed

title: Implement trigger-reality drift detection in doctor
description:
Trigger-reality drift: a commandPattern or pathPattern was accurate when written but no longer matches current usage (command renamed, path restructured). The lesson compiles and passes doctor but silently never fires.

Detection requires fire-rate tracking infrastructure: lessons need a 'lastFiredAt' timestamp and 'fireCount' updated at inject time. Doctor can then flag lessons that have matched trigger conditions zero times in the past N sessions.

Prerequisites:
- Add fireCount/lastFiredAt fields to the lessons schema (migration needed)
- Update the pretooluse hook to increment fireCount on match
- Add doctor check: hint/guard with fireCount=0 and age > 30 days → 'possible trigger drift, has never fired'

This was deferred from ll-pst because it requires runtime execution data, not static analysis.

title: Improve solution-staleness detection beyond version string heuristic
description:
Current staleness check (doctor check #10) only flags solutions containing version strings like @1.2.3 or v5. This misses:
- Renamed CLI flags (e.g. --no-verify deprecated in newer git)
- Deprecated API patterns (e.g. removed SDK methods)
- File paths or config keys that were restructured

Potential approaches:
1. LLM-assisted check: batch active lessons through a fast model, ask 'is this still accurate guidance?'
2. Date-based flag: lessons older than N months with no edits get a review-recommended flag
3. Community/manual: periodic manual review sessions for lessons by tag

This was deferred from ll-pst because it requires either execution data, an LLM call budget, or community tooling.
close_reason: Extended solution-staleness to cover problem field. Added temporal-language check (deprecated/removed in/formerly/was renamed/no longer supported/as of YYYY). Added stale-lesson check (>180 days, age in days). 6 new tests (37 total). Caught 2 real lessons. Commit 5a7bc8f.

title: Implement leval CLI — lesson injection eval harness
description:
Build leval, a CLI eval harness that tests all 95 lessons in the store (vs. the 9 hand-crafted Promptfoo scenarios). Complements the existing Promptfoo framework by adding breadth coverage.

Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-09-lesson-eval-harness-design.md (approved, with 12 inline feedback comments)
Plan: ~/.claude/plans/lessons-learned-session-toasty-stallman.md

Key design:
- CLI: leval gen | run [--phase 1|2|all] | runs | report
- Phase 1 (mechanical): subprocess hook tests by lesson type
- Phase 2 (behavioral): real CC agent via claude -p, LLM judge with 3 prompt forms
- SQLite history in evals/data/eval.db
- Auto-generates missing trigger prompts via Claude API
- Judge detects control_correct before running treatment
- Reuse evals/providers/claude-agent.mjs isolation pattern

Blocker: Finish Tier 2+3 on existing Promptfoo framework first.
close_reason: Superseded by the Promptfoo-native Tier 2+3 eval framework design (session 2026-05-10). The leval CLI approach would have duplicated Promptfoo's built-in SQLite persistence, web viewer, MCP server, and run history. The new design extends Promptfoo natively instead. All design decisions locked in the approved plan at ~/.claude/plans/lessons-learned-session-giggly-beacon.md.

title: Add shell gotcha lessons: osascript dollar-sign mangling and LaunchAgent HOME env var
description: Two lessons ready to ingest at ~/.claude/handoffs/shell-gotchas-for-lessons-learned.md. Each is formatted as a #lesson tag and can be pasted directly into a lessons-learned session. Lessons: (1) osascript heredoc $ mangling — shell vars inside osascript/Python string interpolation need escaped $ or single-quoted heredoc delimiter. (2) LaunchAgent HOME env var — launchd does not inherit shell env; always set HOME explicitly in EnvironmentVariables plist dict.
close_reason: Added both lessons via CLI: osascript-heredoc-dollar-signs-are-expan-5765 and launchd-agents-do-not-inherit-shell-env-3142. Manifest rebuilt, 97 lessons total, all 175 unit tests pass.

title: Audit and document cross-tool compatibility (Codex, Gemini, Kimi, OpenClaw, etc.)
description: Map which plugin features require Claude Code specifically vs can run in any agent environment: hooks (SessionStart, PreToolUse, SubagentStart), session-start injection output, #lesson tag scanning, CLI (lessons.mjs), lesson-manifest.json format. Identify gaps and what would be needed to support each platform. Document a compatibility matrix. Cross-reference lessons-learned-a3v (community engagement) — cross-tool compatibility is a prerequisite for broader community adoption.

title: Spike: SQLite vector embeddings for lesson detection in transcript scanner
description:
Explore adding a vector column (sqlite-vec or sqlite-vss) to lessons.db to embed session transcript messages and detect lesson-worthy moments semantically. Use as supplement or replacement for heuristic Tier 2 scanning.

Key dimension: vector/embedding approach is likely significantly more token-conservative than current heuristics — embeddings + similarity search do heavy lifting without feeding large transcript chunks to the model. This is a major advantage for keeping scanning fast and cheap.

Evaluate: accuracy vs current heuristics, false positive rate, embedding model options (local vs API, cost vs quality tradeoff), token cost reduction vs current Tier 2, integration with CLI tooling to minimize context consumption. Could also surface pre-tag-era lessons from old logs that heuristics missed.
close_reason: Semantic scanner implemented and validated. Tier 3 confirmed better than Tier 2: no false-positive terminal dump candidates, only semantically close windows trigger Claude. Three bugs fixed post-implementation: context overflow, threshold calibration (0.8→0.72), abort on consecutive Claude failures.

title: Evaluate merging protocol and directive lesson types; add per-type intro paragraphs
description: Audit whether protocol and directive lesson types are meaningfully distinct or effectively redundant. Decide: merge into one type with clear semantics, or keep both with explicit distinguishing criteria documented. Either way, add a short introductory paragraph before each type group in session-start output to orient the model on how to interpret each group.

title: Add 5 additional anti-pattern checks to doctor/preflight (trigger-reality drift, staleness, overspecified trigger, context bleed, priority homogeneity)
description:
Five additional lesson quality anti-patterns identified that have no current automated check. Each needs a detection strategy and implementation in bd doctor / bd lint.

Anti-patterns:
1. Trigger-reality drift: commandPattern/pathPattern was accurate when written but no longer matches current usage (command renamed, path restructured). Lesson technically compiles but never fires.
2. Solution staleness: solution references an API, flag, or option that no longer exists. Injects actively wrong guidance.
3. Overspecified trigger: pattern matches only one invocation form, missing equivalents (e.g., 'git stash' but not 'git stash push'). Fires rarely in practice.
4. Context bleed: problem/solution uses session-specific shorthand only parseable if you were in the original session. Confusing to the model and to reviewers.
5. Priority homogeneity: many lessons at same priority+type — injection ordering within the cluster is arbitrary.

Some of these (staleness, drift) require execution data (fire rate tracking) rather than static analysis. File sub-issues as the approach for each becomes clear.

See docs/architecture/quality-checks.md for full anti-pattern catalog. Depends on: lessons-learned-4ef (baseline checks), lessons-learned-nwe (intake validation).
close_reason: Preflight command added to scripts/lessons.mjs (wraps doctor + manifest freshness check). Overspecified trigger, context bleed, and priority homogeneity were already implemented in doctor. Filed ll-9vy (trigger-reality drift) and ll-wer (advanced staleness) as deferred sub-issues requiring runtime execution data.

title: Document manual lesson entry by type with concrete examples
description:
People entering lessons manually (via lessons add or lessons edit --patch) don't know which fields are required per type, or that some field combinations are contradictory (e.g., directive+toolNames).

Create a doc (e.g., docs/contributing-lessons.md or expand CLAUDE.md) with:
- One concrete example per lesson type (hint, guard, directive, protocol)
- The directive vs hint distinction explained with a before/after showing the toolNames pitfall
- Which fields are required vs optional per type
- What commandMatchTarget: 'executable' does and when to use it
- Common mistakes and how to avoid them

Extend this to also cover best practices for:
- Capturing lessons: what makes a good lesson, when to emit #lesson tags, how to frame problem/solution for maximum compliance
- Reviewing and promoting candidates: how to evaluate quality, spot duplicates, decide priority
- Optimizing existing lessons: how to tune summaries, triggers, and patterns for better compliance and fewer false positives

This is a companion to the intake validation work (lessons-learned-nwe) — documentation prevents problems that can't yet be caught by automated checks.
close_reason: Created docs/contributing-lessons.md with: one concrete example per type, directive vs hint toolNames pitfall, field requirements table, commandMatchTarget explanation, dead trigger trap, and best practices for capturing/reviewing/tuning lessons.

title: Investigate intake and lifecycle validation to catch anti-patterns at earliest possible stage
description:
Anti-patterns that exist in the lesson store are harder to fix than ones caught on entry. This issue tracks investigating where to add validation at each lifecycle stage.

Anti-patterns to prevent (full reference in docs/architecture/quality-checks.md):

1. directive+toolNames misconfiguration: lessons add / lessons edit should warn or block if type=directive and toolNames is non-empty (toolNames is silently ignored for directives — should be hint instead)
2. Dead trigger: hint/guard with commandPatterns or pathPatterns but no toolNames — will never fire
3. Weak problem-solution pair: solution word-count floor + high word-overlap ratio with problem (solution just restates problem)
4. Global scope for narrow concept: heuristic — flag when tags or summary suggest domain-specific knowledge but scope is null
5. Project-type/content-aware detection mechanisms: future — detect repo type at inject time to scope lessons dynamically

Lifecycle stages to add checks:
- lessons add (intake) — block or warn
- scan promote — same checks before promoting candidates
- lessons edit (patch) — re-validate affected fields
- bd lint / bd doctor — batch audit of existing store
- bd preflight — pre-PR gate

Goal: prevent anti-patterns from entering the store rather than discovering them in post-hoc audits.
close_reason: Added 3 new checks to validateLesson(): directive+toolNames misconfiguration, dead-trigger (hint/guard with patterns but no toolNames), and weak-pair (solution < 60 chars or Jaccard >= 0.7 similarity with problem). Also moved WEAK_SOLUTION_MIN_LENGTH and SOLUTION_RESTATE_THRESHOLD constants up to validation section so both validateLesson and auditLesson share them.

title: Identify ways to engage the community in building this plugin
description:
This plugin has value beyond a single user's session history. Community involvement could:

- Contribute lessons from their own sessions (curated lesson packs)
- Propose and implement injection strategies for nursery archetypes
- Add support for new lesson types or matching mechanisms
- Share project-type-specific lesson sets (e.g., Django dev, Kubernetes, etc.)

What to figure out:
- Best format for submitting community lessons (PR? issue template? lesson pack file?)
- How to curate/review community submissions without quality degrading
- What contribution docs / contributing.md need to say
- Should there be a community lessons registry separate from personal lessons.db?

A GitHub issue should be opened alongside this beads issue to make the invitation public and gather external ideas — especially around the nursery problem (lessons that exist but have no viable trigger).

title: Study two unresolved lesson injection archetypes: topic/content-aware and project-keyword-matching
description: Two injection patterns were identified but not implemented: (1) topic/content-aware -- inject lessons based on what Claude is writing about, not just which tool is called; (2) project-keyword-matching -- match lessons against project-specific terms (e.g. beads, dolt) regardless of tool. Need to study feasibility, hook API surface, and whether these are best modeled as lesson types or matcher extensions.

title: Add lesson quality audit checks to doctor/preflight command
description:
doctor and preflight currently check sync state and orphans. They don't validate lesson quality: missing toolNames (lesson can never fire), summary over 80 chars (truncation), placeholder text left in fields, or zero-match lessons. Adding these checks would surface dead lessons before they waste injection budget.

Also extend to include a protocol message audit: examine the #lesson tag emission instructions injected at session start (session-start-lesson-protocol.mjs). Audit for compliance rate and coverage — are lessons being emitted for a wide range of problem/solution sequences, or are they only triggered in narrow cases? Iteratively improve the instruction framing to increase compliance and detect a wider range of lessons. Measure improvement by scanning session logs before/after.
close_reason: Implemented lessons doctor subcommand with 5 checks (missing toolNames, summary >80 chars, truncated summary, template placeholders, no patterns). 14 integration tests, all green.

title: Doctor weekly scheduling via CronCreate
description: Weekly automated doctor audit is missing. The lessons:doctor command and its auto-trigger after lessons:review are implemented, but there is no weekly schedule. Implement using CronCreate to schedule a recurring Claude Code session that runs /lessons:doctor and optionally reports results.

title: E2E tests and config: resume incomplete test/config work from session f50e54c8
description: Session f50e54c8 (moonlit-bubbling-deer) examined config.json, E2E tests, and CLI tools across multiple 'continue' cycles without confirmed completion. Needs: read last 200 lines of session to identify exact stopping point, verify current test pass rate, identify specific config or E2E gaps left open.
close_reason: Fixed suppressOutput schema key in E2E test and implemented tag-grouped headers in lessons review. All 269 tests pass.

title: New lesson type: knowledge/model override for correcting Claude's built-in assumptions
description: Session a8f370e2 designed a new lesson type that lets users override or tune Claude's internal knowledge and default skill behaviors. Example use case: correcting superpowers:creating-plans behavior in a way that persists across skill upgrades. Design was in discovery phase when interrupted. Needs: finalized type spec, schema changes, injection mechanics, and a worked example.
close_reason: Archived: new lesson type design deferred; existing hint/guard/protocol/directive taxonomy is sufficient for current needs

title: Configure cloud-init for each cluster node
description: Run pi-cloud-init for pi-1, pi-2, pi-3. Copy output to each node boot partition. Verify cloud-init status on first PXE boot.
close_reason: All three complete: EEPROM 0xf21 confirmed on pi5-3, PXE booting as pi-3, cloud-init status done

title: Install k3s: server on pi1, agents on pi2+pi3
description: Install k3s server on pi-1. Capture node token. Install k3s agent on pi-2 and pi-3 pointing at pi-1:6443. Verify with kubectl get nodes showing all 3 Ready.

title: MCP server: complete brainstorm → implementation plan
description: Brainstorming session for the MCP server feature (PRD at docs/prds/003-mcp-server.md) was interrupted mid-session. Need to: (1) finish the brainstorming Q&A (proxy diagram was the last open question, user understood it), (2) write a proper implementation plan (not a PRD copy) in the plan file, (3) call ExitPlanMode so user can comment inline and approve. Key decisions already made: transparent proxy architecture, sibling mcp/ package with own package.json, shared data/ directory (SQLite + manifest), Desktop Commander tool name mapping via normalize-tool.mjs.

title: Brainstorm lesson injection system from first principles
description: The current injection system uses 4 types: hint (trigger-based), guard (block-on-trigger), protocol (session-start), directive. Two issues surfaced during the lesson audit: (1) 11 hint lessons had no triggers and were effectively dead, requiring reclassification; (2) trigger-based injection only works for tool/command patterns — there is no support for task-contextual injection (e.g., 'relevant when planning a batch') or multi-level injection (session-start + pre-tool). This task is to brainstorm from first principles: what are all the moments where lesson injection can affect agent behavior, and what injection model best covers them? Topics: task-contextual triggers, SubagentStart injection, injection at plan entry, combining triggers with session-start for the same lesson, and whether summary should be computed from problem rather than stored separately.

title: Redundancy sweep: make type the authoritative signal everywhere
description:
After the type taxonomy PR stabilises, audit all interfaces to ensure type is the single source of truth across the injection pipeline. A developer should only need to understand type to understand full injection behavior.

Scope:
- manifest keys/shape — ensure no legacy fields leak through
- hook branching — all conditional logic should key off type, not derived booleans
- CLI output (list, add confirmation) — show type prominently
- docs (architecture/data-model.md) — reflect new taxonomy
- any remaining references to injectOn, block, blockReason, or injection in comments/docs
close_reason: All changes implemented: sessionStart removed from manifest+hook, legacy patchable fields removed from db.mjs, manifest schema fixed (type enum + message field), docs updated with type taxonomy, tests all pass

title: Architectural eval: unified ecosystem map of evaluation frameworks
description: Two parallel sessions (86d95763, cc89996a) both attempted to create a unified ecosystem map across 4 architectural evaluation documents — listing each tool/framework with what it does, strengths, limitations, surfacing model, and relevance to PRDs. Both interrupted before completion. Context recovery needed before resuming.
